title: The One with the Birthing Video

jokes.text: Something really boring happened to someone ugly in the Middle Ages.
jokes.setup: Rachel complaining about Ross making them watch Discovery Channel
jokes.punchline: Overly dismissive, reductive description of educational programming
jokes.explanation: Efficient comedic description that captures how boring educational TV can seem. Good economy and structure.

jokes.text: That is one lucky to-go cup of coffee.
jokes.setup: Gunther watching Rachel leave with coffee
jokes.punchline: Expressing jealousy toward an inanimate object Rachel is touching
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gunther character moment - his unrequited love for Rachel expressed through absurd jealousy of coffee cup.

jokes.text: Is there anything I can do for you? Do you wanna look down my top?
jokes.setup: Phoebe trying to help sad Joey
jokes.punchline: Offering to flash him as comfort, treating it casually
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Phoebe - offering something inappropriate as if it's perfectly normal help.

jokes.text: Thanks. Maybe later.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's offer to let him look down her top
jokes.punchline: Politely declining as if it's a normal offer he might take up later
jokes.explanation: Joey's casual response treats Phoebe's inappropriate offer as mundane, creating absurd normalcy.

jokes.text: I want a lot of things. I wanna be with the woman I love! I want her to love me back. And I want relief from the wrenching pain of knowing that won't happen!
jokes.setup: Gunther asking if Joey wants anything
jokes.punchline: Joey launches into dramatic monologue about unrequited love instead of ordering
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from simple question to overwrought emotional outburst, though slightly predictable.

jokes.text: We have red bagels.
jokes.setup: Joey's dramatic speech about unrequited love and pain
jokes.punchline: Gunther responds with mundane menu item as if that addresses Joey's emotional needs
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan response. Perfect economy - two words that completely deflate Joey's drama. Classic comedy timing.

jokes.text: Okay.
jokes.setup: Gunther's absurd suggestion that red bagels solve his love problems
jokes.punchline: Joey accepts this non-solution with resignation
jokes.explanation: Perfect cap to the sequence - Joey's defeated acceptance of bagels as emotional solution.

jokes.text: There was a drumstick in the couch.
jokes.setup: Ross denying he's uptight about crumbs
jokes.punchline: Joey reveals the specific gross thing he left that justified Ross's concern
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Joey's slovenliness while justifying Ross's 'uptight' behavior.

jokes.text: And that's how the plague started. If you wanna relive that again...
jokes.setup: Ross explaining why crumbs attract mice and mice leave droppings
jokes.punchline: Dramatically connecting Joey's messiness to historical catastrophe
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross character comedy - taking minor domestic issue to ridiculous historical extreme with academic authority.

jokes.text: Mice didn't start the plague, rats did. 'Rattus norvegicus', to be exact.
jokes.setup: Ross's dramatic plague reference
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself with pedantic correction including Latin name
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ross moment - undermines his own dramatic point with compulsive academic precision. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: Oh, good God, Ross. Nick at Nite, once in a while.
jokes.setup: Ross's pedantic correction about plague rats
jokes.punchline: Joey suggesting Ross needs to watch mindless TV instead of being so educational
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic - Joey advocating for lowbrow entertainment as antidote to Ross's intellectualism.

jokes.text: She even got the baby a T-shirt that says 'Fossils are my friends'.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining how supportive Mona is
jokes.punchline: The baby gift reveals how she's trying too hard to connect with Ross's paleontology work
jokes.explanation: Cute character detail showing Mona's attempts to appeal to Ross through his professional interests.

jokes.text: Come on, Mona. Don't kiss ass.
jokes.setup: Ross describing Mona's thoughtful baby gift
jokes.punchline: Joey calling out the obvious brown-nosing behavior
jokes.explanation: Direct observation that cuts through Ross's obliviousness about Mona's obvious pandering.

jokes.text: Candy's the mom, Cookie's the daughter. The father's name is also Cookie.
jokes.setup: Explaining the confusing names on the birthing video
jokes.punchline: The father also being named Cookie makes an already silly situation more absurd
jokes.explanation: Absurd naming coincidence that escalates the silliness. Good structure but not character-specific.

jokes.text: Why am I friends with these people?
jokes.setup: The absurd Cookie family naming situation
jokes.punchline: Ross questioning his life choices based on his friends' ridiculous names
jokes.explanation: Good character moment of Ross's judgment of others, though relatively mild.

jokes.text: Monica, grow up.
jokes.setup: Monica wanting to show him something in the bathroom (implied lingerie)
jokes.punchline: Chandler acting above sexual excitement while clearly interested
jokes.explanation: Weak joke - predictable married couple dynamic without much wit or surprise.

jokes.text: You don't want my opinion? Not really.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking why Monica doesn't want his opinion
jokes.punchline: Monica's blunt honesty about not valuing his input on lingerie
jokes.explanation: Decent relationship humor - Monica's honesty about gender differences in fashion opinions.

jokes.text: Which one would make your best friend want to do your little sister?
jokes.setup: Ross asking her to get his opinion as her big brother
jokes.punchline: Monica framing the question in the most uncomfortable way possible for Ross
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy - Monica deliberately making Ross uncomfortable by framing lingerie choice in brother/sister terms.

jokes.text: The red one.
jokes.setup: Monica's deliberately uncomfortable question about which lingerie would make Chandler want to sleep with her
jokes.punchline: Ross answers immediately and matter-of-factly despite the awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response - Ross's immediate practical answer ignores all the awkwardness Monica created.

jokes.text: Right outside is a real, live, furry playmate.
jokes.setup: Phoebe saying she brought someone to cheer up Joey
jokes.punchline: Description sounds sexual but she's talking about a dog
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection setup - description could be sexual but reveals innocent dog. Competent but predictable.

jokes.text: I'm not sleeping with your friend Jane again.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's offer of a 'furry playmate'
jokes.punchline: Joey assumes it's another setup with unattractive friend Jane
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Joey's history with Phoebe's questionable setups. Sets up next joke nicely.

jokes.text: Who, you gotta admit, looks a lot like Jane.
jokes.setup: Revealing the 'furry playmate' is actually a dog
jokes.punchline: Phoebe doubling down by saying the dog resembles unattractive Jane
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that adds insult to injury - not only is Jane unattractive, she looks like a dog.

jokes.text: I borrow him when I get really depressed about world hunger or poverty. Not to trivialize your pain.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining she borrows the dog when depressed
jokes.punchline: She trivializes Joey's heartbreak by comparing it to global suffering while claiming not to
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe character comedy - accidentally insulting while trying to be supportive, with complete obliviousness.

jokes.text: Oh, not that kind of fun.
jokes.setup: Joey excitedly saying he and the dog will have 'so much fun'
jokes.punchline: Phoebe clarifying he shouldn't have sex with the dog
jokes.explanation: Cheap shock humor - relies on bestiality reference without much craft. Economy is good but content is lazy.

jokes.text: I'm slipping into something a bit less comfortable and a bit more slutty.
jokes.setup: Monica preparing for Valentine's Day with Chandler
jokes.punchline: Inverting the cliche 'something more comfortable' to emphasize sexual purpose over comfort
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that subverts common phrase. Good economy and quotable delivery.

jokes.text: Monica got me porn? Girl-on-girl porn?! She really must love me!
jokes.setup: Chandler finding the 'Candy and Cookie' tape
jokes.punchline: Misinterpreting birthing video as pornography, then lesbian porn, then proof of Monica's love
jokes.explanation: Good escalating misunderstanding with solid structure. Each assumption gets more wrong and more excited.

jokes.text: Worst porn ever! Worst porn ever!
jokes.setup: Chandler realizing the 'porn' is actually a birthing video
jokes.punchline: Complete reversal from excitement to horror, repeated for emphasis
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the misdirection setup. The repetition emphasizes his horror at the misunderstanding.

jokes.text: You're cute, but you're not too smart.
jokes.setup: Joey playing fetch with the dog
jokes.punchline: Talking to the dog like it's a dim date rather than just being a normal dog
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Joey treating the dog like one of his shallow romantic partners.

jokes.text: Did I just throw this?
jokes.setup: Joey trying to play fetch with the dog
jokes.punchline: Joey forgetting he threw the ball, showing he might be the dumb one
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - calling the dog stupid then immediately proving he's the dim one. Good ironic reversal.

jokes.text: I don't know his real name. I call him Mozzarella.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking about the dog
jokes.punchline: Joey naming the dog after cheese instead of learning its actual name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey character moment - food-obsessed and lazy about learning actual names. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: I hope I stop talking like this before my meeting. Yes, I do. Bye-bye, Joey. Seriously, I can't stop it.
jokes.setup: Rachel baby-talking to the cute dog
jokes.punchline: She's stuck in baby-talk mode and panicking about professional consequences
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of a relatable problem - getting stuck in inappropriate speech patterns at wrong times.

jokes.text: That's Rachel. She used to live here. Might as well be honest with you. We love her. But we can't have her.
jokes.setup: Joey introducing the dog to the situation
jokes.punchline: Joey explaining his unrequited love to a dog like it's relationship therapy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Joey using the dog as a confidant for his romantic problems. Sets up his depression transfer.

jokes.text: Well, you used to be.
jokes.setup: Joey telling the dog 'you understand, right? You're a guy'
jokes.punchline: Casually referencing the dog's neutering
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered casually. Good economy and unexpected turn that adds layer to the dog therapy session.

jokes.text: It's yelling, bleeding, dilating. Oh, the dilating!
jokes.setup: Monica asking what's wrong with the birthing video
jokes.punchline: Chandler's increasingly horrified description of childbirth, emphasizing the dilation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of male squeamishness about childbirth. The dilation emphasis adds extra cringe.

jokes.text: If you thought I was gonna get you porn for Valentine's Day... you were right.
jokes.setup: Monica seeming shocked that Chandler expected porn
jokes.punchline: Reveals she actually did get him porn, just not the birthing video
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with strong structure. Sets up expectation then delivers opposite but in expected direction.

jokes.text: It's about a girl who moves to the city in search of stardom... but ends up having sex with a lot of guys.
jokes.setup: Monica describing the porn plot
jokes.punchline: Treating porn plot as if it's a legitimate dramatic narrative
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from treating porn like serious cinema, but pretty predictable territory.

jokes.text: Wait a minute. Those aren't stars.
jokes.setup: Monica saying the porn 'got four stars'
jokes.punchline: Realizing the 'stars' are actually a different rating system (likely explicit content warnings)
jokes.explanation: Decent visual gag about misunderstanding porn rating systems. Good timing but not especially memorable.

jokes.text: Remember the first time you saw 'Jaws'... how long it took to go back in the water?
jokes.setup: Chandler being traumatized by the birthing video
jokes.punchline: Comparing childbirth trauma to shark movie trauma with water phobia
jokes.explanation: Clever analogy comparing birthing video trauma to classic movie trauma. Good cultural reference with solid structure.

jokes.text: Otherwise, the horse will go change back into sweatpants.
jokes.setup: Monica's 'get back on the horse' relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Extending the metaphor to Monica literally changing out of sexy clothes if they don't have sex
jokes.explanation: Creative extended metaphor that works on multiple levels. Good absurdist imagery with practical relationship stakes.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! No wonder my mother hates me!
jokes.setup: Monica watching the birthing video
jokes.punchline: Realizing the trauma of her own birth explains her mother's attitude toward her
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark callback that recontextualizes Monica's family dynamics through birthing horror. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Interestingly, it isn't made with seawater. It's actually made with salted fresh water.
jokes.setup: Mona giving Ross saltwater taffy as a gift
jokes.punchline: Ross immediately correcting the name with unnecessary pedantic detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross character moment - unable to accept a simple gift without providing unwanted educational content.

jokes.text: That's not interesting.
jokes.setup: Ross's pedantic explanation about saltwater taffy
jokes.punchline: Mona directly contradicting Ross's claim that his fact was interesting
jokes.explanation: Good straight-woman response to Ross's pedantry. Simple but effective shutdown.

jokes.text: I think it's interesting. Me too. I missed you!
jokes.setup: Mona saying Ross's fact wasn't interesting
jokes.punchline: Ross doubling down, agreeing with himself, then desperately changing subject
jokes.explanation: Good character escalation - Ross's inability to let go of being 'right' followed by awkward topic change.

jokes.text: Joey cracks me up. 'Have your ex-wife move in. That wouldn't be awkward.'
jokes.setup: Mona thinking Joey's suggestion was a joke
jokes.punchline: She's describing exactly what actually happened while thinking it's absurd
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - audience knows she's describing the actual situation while thinking it's ridiculous.

jokes.text: Imagine, I go away for a few days... and my boyfriend is living with a woman he got pregnant.
jokes.setup: Mona continuing to describe the 'ridiculous' scenario
jokes.punchline: She's literally describing her actual situation while Ross squirms
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic irony escalation - each detail makes the situation more uncomfortable for Ross and audience.

jokes.text: My mom was too busy planning her suicide to provide saltwater treats.
jokes.setup: Ross asking if Phoebe has ever had saltwater taffy
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning her mother's suicidal tendencies as explanation for missing childhood experiences
jokes.explanation: Excellent Phoebe moment - dark childhood trauma delivered with complete casualness about something as trivial as candy.

jokes.text: I'll tell her my pregnant ex-girlfriend is living with me.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining his plan to butter Mona up before telling the truth
jokes.punchline: The casual way he describes the objectively terrible situation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe setup - Ross's obliviousness to how bad his situation sounds when stated plainly.

jokes.text: If I haven't said it before, she's a lucky, lucky lady.
jokes.setup: Ross describing his plan to tell Mona about Rachel
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's sarcastic assessment of Mona's 'good fortune' in dating Ross
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response that highlights how terrible Ross's relationship behavior is.

jokes.text: What the crap is up with this stuff? Is it gum? Is it food? What's the deal?
jokes.setup: Phoebe trying saltwater taffy for the first time
jokes.punchline: Confused escalation about the ambiguous nature of taffy texture
jokes.explanation: Relatable observation about taffy's confusing texture, delivered with Phoebe's characteristic bluntness.

jokes.text: Last year Greg took me to sushi... then we trashed a nearby research lab and freed 20 monkeys.
jokes.setup: Ross asking about Phoebe's best date
jokes.punchline: Casual description escalates from normal sushi date to criminal animal liberation
jokes.explanation: Excellent Phoebe escalation - starts with normal date activity then casually describes eco-terrorism. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: I knew it. I knew I wasn't crazy.
jokes.setup: Phoebe mentioning freeing research monkeys
jokes.punchline: Ross realizes this explains a previous monkey attack he experienced
jokes.explanation: Good callback that connects Phoebe's past crime to Ross's unexplained experience. Nice continuity building.

jokes.text: Last year a monkey did attack me at the bus stop.
jokes.setup: Ross's realization about Phoebe's monkey liberation
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact description of being attacked by escaped research monkey
jokes.explanation: Good followup that explains the random monkey attack as consequence of Phoebe's activism.

jokes.text: It'd be like you falling in love with a cat.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining why she and Ross can't be together
jokes.punchline: Comparing romantic impossibility to Ross's known hatred of cats
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific analogy that uses established Ross trait to illustrate romantic impossibility.

jokes.text: Oh, me, right.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking Joey to excuse them so she can talk to the dog
jokes.punchline: Joey realizing he's the one being dismissed, not the dog
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective moment of Joey's confusion about being the one dismissed rather than the dog.

jokes.text: He's breathing.
jokes.setup: Phoebe pointing out how depressed the formerly happy dog has become
jokes.punchline: Describing basic life function as evidence of the dog's misery
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery - treating basic breathing as barely adequate sign of life shows how depressed the dog is.

jokes.text: God, what have I done to you?
jokes.setup: Joey realizing he's transferred his depression to the happiest dog in the world
jokes.punchline: Joey's guilt over psychologically damaging an innocent animal
jokes.explanation: Good emotional escalation showing Joey's realization of his psychological impact on the dog.

jokes.text: I broke the dog, Pheebs! I broke the dog!
jokes.setup: Joey's guilt over the dog's depression
jokes.punchline: Dramatic confession treating the dog like a broken appliance
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Joey treating psychological damage like physical breakage. Quotable delivery.

jokes.text: Interestingly, it isn't made with seawater...
jokes.setup: Rachel mentioning saltwater taffy
jokes.punchline: Starting to repeat Ross's exact pedantic explanation
jokes.explanation: Decent callback showing Ross's pedantry is infectious, though not as strong as original.

jokes.text: We actually watched the documentary together.
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining how she learned the taffy fact
jokes.punchline: Revealing Ross made her watch an entire documentary about candy manufacturing
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reveals the extent of Ross's documentary obsession - even candy requires full educational treatment.

jokes.text: And by emotional, I mean crazy!
jokes.setup: Ross telling Mona that Rachel is 'emotional'
jokes.punchline: Immediately clarifying he means actually mentally unstable
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Ross's desperation to explain away Rachel's presence without revealing truth.

jokes.text: Yes, she's emotional, but ballsy.
jokes.setup: Mona asking about Rachel having food delivered
jokes.punchline: Ross framing Rachel's normal behavior as courageously boundary-crossing
jokes.explanation: Decent moment of Ross trying to spin normal behavior as impressive to avoid revealing the truth.

jokes.text: And you thought she was gonna be in our way!
jokes.setup: Rachel announcing she's going to bed in sweatpants
jokes.punchline: Ross's sarcastic comment to Mona while Rachel acts exactly like she lives there
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment as Ross tries to deflect while Rachel's behavior proves exactly what Mona suspected.

jokes.text: You got another ex-wife back there?
jokes.setup: Ross saying he has a surprise
jokes.punchline: Mona's sarcastic question about more hidden romantic complications
jokes.explanation: Sharp sarcastic response that shows Mona's growing awareness of Ross's pattern of hidden complications.

jokes.text: But, Mona, I live here.
jokes.setup: Mona telling Rachel to go back to her own place
jokes.punchline: Rachel revealing the truth Mona didn't know
jokes.explanation: Perfect revelation moment - simple delivery of the bombshell that destroys Ross's deception. Great structure.

jokes.text: Till now, the worst thing I'd seen was my dad doing shots off the houseboy.
jokes.setup: Chandler describing how the birthing video haunts him
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of his father's sexual behavior with household staff
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy callback that puts birthing video in perspective through even more disturbing family history.

jokes.text: After this, I would gladly make that my screen saver.
jokes.setup: Chandler describing his father doing shots off the houseboy
jokes.punchline: Willing to make traumatic family scene his computer background to avoid birthing imagery
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that emphasizes how traumatic the birthing video was by comparison to family dysfunction.

jokes.text: We watch him do yoga in his underwear, but for this he closes the drapes.
jokes.setup: Ross and Mona fighting behind closed drapes
jokes.punchline: Pointing out Ross's inconsistent modesty - fine with underwear yoga but private about breakup
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about Ross's selective privacy - revealing about exercise but hiding emotional drama.

jokes.text: It's ironic how footage of someone being born... can make you want to kill yourself.
jokes.setup: Warning Phoebe about the birthing video
jokes.punchline: Observing the contradiction between life-giving process and death-inducing reaction
jokes.explanation: Sharp ironic observation about the contradiction between birth as life-affirming versus psychologically traumatic.

jokes.text: But the stork is gonna bring mine.
jokes.setup: Monica saying Phoebe wants to have kids
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's childlike belief that she can avoid childbirth through magical delivery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - combining maternal desire with magical thinking to avoid biological reality.

jokes.text: But you leave a drumstick under there and you're a bad roommate.
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler hiding under the coffee table from birthing video
jokes.punchline: Referencing Joey's food mess while they're doing something equally ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Good callback that ironically compares Joey's food mess to their current childish hiding behavior.

jokes.text: Why is that baby torturing that woman?
jokes.setup: Phoebe watching the birthing video
jokes.punchline: Interpreting childbirth as deliberate malicious action by the baby
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe observation that personifies the baby as actively malicious rather than passively being born.

jokes.text: Did her ass explode?
jokes.setup: Rachel telling them to look at something happening in the video
jokes.punchline: Chandler's assumption that any development must be more horrifying
jokes.explanation: Crude humor that fits Chandler's traumatized state, though relies on shock value more than wit.

jokes.text: I didn't tell her I got Rachel pregnant. I gave her a key to my apartment and then had the locks changed. Then I lied to her about Rachel moving in with me.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining why Mona broke up with him
jokes.punchline: Cataloguing his increasingly terrible relationship behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure listing Ross's relationship failures in ascending order of awfulness.

jokes.text: In a way, I judge her for not breaking up with me sooner.
jokes.setup: Ross listing his terrible relationship behavior
jokes.punchline: Blaming Mona for tolerating his bad behavior too long
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Ross's complete inability to accept responsibility, turning his failings into criticism of her.

jokes.text: You know, actually, once I got dumped during sex.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to relate to Joey's relationship problems
jokes.punchline: Oversharing an extremely awkward personal detail
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Ross's attempt at empathy through inappropriate oversharing of humiliating experience.

jokes.text: She's not a Tribbiani?
jokes.setup: Joey saying he likes a woman but it can't happen
jokes.punchline: Ross's first assumption is that Joey's own family genetics are the problem
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Joey's family's questionable genetics as romantic obstacle. Character-appropriate assumption from Ross.

jokes.text: I only say that... so Chandler won't feel threatened because I'm his only friend.
jokes.setup: Joey claiming he doesn't hate everyone at work
jokes.punchline: Revealing his workplace negativity is actually to protect Chandler's feelings
jokes.explanation: Excellent character revelation showing Joey's emotional intelligence in protecting Chandler's self-esteem.

jokes.text: Sorry. And I'm your only friend too.
jokes.setup: Joey explaining his protective behavior toward Chandler
jokes.punchline: Quickly clarifying the friendship exclusivity goes both ways
jokes.explanation: Good followup that maintains the mutual friendship exclusivity while showing Joey's consideration.

jokes.text: I'm gonna put the bourbon in it at home.
jokes.setup: Phoebe bringing Joey warm milk
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning her plan to spike the comfort drink with alcohol
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe moment - treating alcoholism as casual comfort strategy with typical unconventional approach.

jokes.text: It's Rachel.
jokes.setup: Ross encouraging Joey to talk to his friend about the woman he likes
jokes.punchline: Revealing the woman Joey loves is Rachel
jokes.explanation: Perfect episode-ending revelation with maximum dramatic impact. Economy and structure are flawless for cliffhanger.

jokes.text: I wonder which one of these guys becomes the father?
jokes.setup: Rachel watching the actual porn Monica got Chandler
jokes.punchline: Treating porn plot as if paternity is the interesting mystery
jokes.explanation: Decent ironic observation treating porn like family drama, though not as sharp as similar earlier jokes.

jokes.text: Nope, can't get pregnant that way.
jokes.setup: Rachel continuing to watch the porn
jokes.punchline: Clinical assessment of sexual acts' reproductive viability
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about treating porn scientifically, though the pregnancy angle is predictable.

tmdb_overview: Chandler and Monica watch a graphic childbirth video. Ross hides Rachel's living situation from Mona.
